code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test12-NOV-2021Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, mild hyperchloremia, and significant proteinuria with low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function. Presence of glucose, bilirubin, and ketones in urine, along with abnormal urine appearance and color, further support possible renal pathology (e.g., glomerular disease or tubular dysfunction). No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further renal workup.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
